name: basic-globe-with-terrain
description: The most fundamental CesiumJS task: create a viewer with world terrain. Tests whether the skill produces correct bootstrapping code that actually renders a globe.
prompt: Create a complete CesiumJS application that shows a 3D globe with world terrain. Use Cesium Ion for the terrain data. The viewer should fill the entire browser window.
expected_behaviors: Sets Ion.defaultAccessToken before creating the Viewer
Creates a new Viewer with a container element
Uses Terrain.fromWorldTerrain() for terrain (preferred over createWorldTerrainAsync)
Includes the required CSS import for widgets
Includes the HTML container div with appropriate sizing
visual_expectations: A full-window 3D globe should be visible with terrain detail (mountains, valleys visible when zoomed in). The default Bing aerial imagery should be the base layer. Standard Cesium widgets (timeline, animation controls, home button, etc.) should be visible around the edges of the viewer.

programmatic_checks.description: No JavaScript errors in the browser console

screenshots.description: After initial globe load and tile fetching

notes: This is the baseline sanity check. If the skill can't produce a working globe, nothing else matters.
